articles.title: Odisha govt considering early Summer Holidays as heatwave intensifies, announcement soon - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Amid prevailing heatwave conditions across Odisha, the state government is likely to advance the summer vacation for schools. A final decision in this regard is expected within the next couple of days.
According to sources, a proposal for early declaration of summer holidays has been submitted to the Chief Minister following detailed discussions between the Revenue Department and the School and Mass Education Department. A joint meeting involving officials from both departments, along with the Special Relief Commissioner (SRC), was held to review the situation arising from the intense heat.
After assessing the ground realities, a formal recommendation has been forwarded to the Chief Minister, urging consideration of early school closure to safeguard students from extreme weather conditions. The Chief Minister is expected to take a final call within one to two days.
Meanwhile, the Revenue Minister has assured that if Anganwadi centres are closed due to the heatwave, necessary arrangements will be made to ensure uninterrupted supply of food to beneficiaries.
Earlier, School and Mass Education Minister Nityananda Gond had stated that the decision on school closures would be based on reports submitted by district Collectors. He had emphasised that districts facing severe and unbearable heat will be assessed individually, and appropriate decisions regarding holidays will be taken accordingly.
The Minister reiterated that the safety and well-being of students remain the government’s top priority.
